ops.title: proc spawn: fd inheritance contract and fork-bomb defense
ops.body:
# The fd-inheritance contract (Spawn ↔ CloseInheritedFDs)

fork+exec hands the child every non-CLOEXEC parent descriptor, and pure Go offers no fork hook to sweep them spawner-side. So any long-lived child binary spawned via proc.Spawn MUST call CloseInheritedFDs before any other work in main — before opening anything non-CLOEXEC. A descriptor whose FD_CLOEXEC flag is unset right after exec can only be one the parent deliberately kept inheritable; the process's own runtime descriptors are CLOEXEC and untouched.

Consequence of skipping it: a consumer session's lease fd — deliberately kept inheritable by the parent — gets pinned for the child's whole lifetime. `proc/closefds_test.go` pins this as incident "P-11": the test acquires a shared lease over a descriptor opened WITHOUT O_CLOEXEC (raw syscall.Open — os.OpenFile force-adds O_CLOEXEC on darwin), spawns, and proves the child no longer pins it after the sweep; the no-sweep case is the negative control proving the leak is real.

**Consumer compliance roster:** fusekit's cmd/holder complies (first call in main).

# Fork-bomb defense (rlimit_darwin.go)

`withChildNprocCap` runs the fork (exec.Cmd.Start) with RLIMIT_NPROC temporarily lowered so the child subtree inherits it: the limit is per real UID, every descendant inherits it, and a runaway re-spawn loop hits EAGAIN instead of exhausting the process table and freezing the machine. It only ever LOWERS the limit; `spawnRlimitMu` ensures no concurrent spawn forks while this process's limit is lowered. `spawnNprocHeadroom` is generous for a holder forking ~0 children yet starves a runaway loop. Off darwin the cap is skipped entirely: Linux RLIMIT_NPROC counts threads, so capping around a fork would starve the Go runtime. This is the code-level counterpart of the 2026-06-24 mount-holder fork-storm incident (cc-pool ccn doc ef281ea) — the same reason tests run only under scripts/test.sh.

# Launch accounting

Spawn.Gate admits each actual child launch (never when Available already holds) and records the attempt durably BEFORE the launch — launch-site accounting; consumers wire drain.StrikeStore.SpawnGate so transition and babysit share one gate and an unaccounted launch is unrepresentable. LaunchStrategy is sealed to ExecLaunch and AppLaunchNew so no strategy can bypass Available/CanHost, the RLIMIT_NPROC cap, or reaping. AppLaunchNew exists because a launchd daemon must never direct-exec a bundle's inner Mach-O: outside the Aqua session, fuse-t volume bring-up and the volume-access TCC grant fail; `open -n -g` lets LaunchServices start it inside the user's session. `reap` waits out the child because Setsid detaches the session, not the parent-child link — an unwaited child strands a zombie.
